timeline.label: Board approved 10% quarterly dividend increase to $0.34 from $0.31 per share
Closed LKC Technologies acquisition (ophthalmic diagnostics); joins Electronic Instruments Group
Closed FARO Technologies acquisition ($933M 2025 deal spend with Kern); 3D measurement platform
Acquired Kern Microtechnik (precision machining and optical inspection)
FY2025 record results: $7.40B sales, $1.67B free cash flow, $3.58B backlog
Completed 15 acquisitions through 2025 (~$1.8B annualized sales) under AMETEK Growth Model
Paragon Medical acquisition integration; medical components scale in EMG
David A. Zapico continues as Chairman and CEO; long-tenured leadership on Growth Model execution

industry.structure: Fragmented niche markets for electronic test instruments, process controls, and precision electromechanical components. AMETEK competes on specification, reliability, and integration capability rather than commodity price. Top positions in targeted sub-niches with mid-20s to high-20s operating margins.
industry.share_shift: Scale serial acquirers (AMETEK, Danaher, Roper, IDEX) continue consolidating small engineered-product businesses. AMETEK gained share via FARO, LKC, and Paragon medical deals while organic growth ran mid-single digits.
industry.trend: Aerospace/defense and medical demand resilient; industrial automation cyclical. Acquisition multiples remain competitive; operational excellence and global sourcing offset inflation. Backlog at record levels supports near-term revenue visibility.
